name: acme-design-system
description: The machine-checkable form of guidelines.md — one policy per guideline section, kept right next to it so you can see how they relate. Each policy's `sql` selects violation rows (SELECT * ... WHERE ...; zero rows = pass); `message` is a template whose {placeholders} are filled from the matched row's columns. How the prose becomes SQL (hand-authored or LLM-compiled behind a deterministic gate) is DOWNSTREAM.md §5; this file is the compiled result.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE value_type = 'color' AND certainty = 'literal' AND norm NOT IN (SELECT norm FROM ref_allowed_colors)
policies.message: Color {norm} on "{property}" is not a design token
policies.help: Use a design-system color token. Unresolved colors are routed to needs-review, never failed here.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE value_type = 'length' AND certainty = 'literal' AND norm NOT IN (SELECT norm FROM ref_allowed_lengths)
policies.message: Length {norm} on "{property}" is not on the spacing scale
policies.help: Use a spacing or radius token (space.*, radius.*) instead of a raw pixel value.

policies.message: Arbitrary Tailwind class "{token}" bypasses the token scale
policies.help: Replace bracketed arbitrary values (e.g. text-[#123456], w-[13px]) with a scale utility.

policies.message: Inline style sets "{property}"; prefer a class or token
policies.help: Move inline styles into a stylesheet or a token-driven class.

policies.sql: SELECT * FROM css_declaration WHERE certainty IN ('dynamic', 'unknown')
policies.help: factlas could not resolve this value statically; a human or a gated Tier-2 check should decide. Never silently passed or failed.
